meta.title: What's happening
meta.note: Curated cross-domain event log — the developments moving Mexico, dated and linked to a first-party or record source. Ranked by importance, then recency. Hand-curated for now; the auto-pipeline (DOF, Banxico, SESNSP, USTR) is the next layer.

events.title: BNP Paribas prevé tres incrementos a la tasa de interés de Banxico para el 2027
events.why: Para este año, se espera que la tasa de interés de Banxico se mantenga sin movimientos en el 6.5%, nivel definido en junio pasado. La publicación BNP Paribas prevé tres incrementos

events.title: Vinci Airports, accionista de OMA, apuesta por México pese a desaceleración del tráfico aéreo
events.why: El CEO de Vinci Airports indicó que espera un crecimiento de tráfico más lento que en años anteriores por el alza en los precios de combustible. La publicación Vinci Airports, acci

events.title: Economía y PNUD alían esfuerzos para impulsar una política industrial con enfoque de desarrollo humano
events.why: Secretaría de Economía y PNUD acuerdan impulsar una política industrial con innovación, inclusión, digitalización y sostenibilidad en México.

events.title: Mexico to file complaints over deaths connected to U.S. immigration enforcement
events.context: Mexico says it will seek criminal complaints over 17 deaths connected to ICE, 14 in custody and three during operations. It will also pursue civil action against private detention operators.
events.why: The sharpest escalation yet in the confrontation with Washington over the deportation crackdown — Mexico will pursue complaints against those responsible for 14 deaths in ICE custody and civil suits against private detention operators.

events.title: Annual inflation falls to 3.37% in June
events.context: The INPC fell 0.27% from May. Annual inflation was 3.37%, down from 4.32% a year earlier.
events.why: A third straight monthly decline, back inside Banxico's 3%±1% band. This is the print that underwrites the pause and keeps real rates high, supporting the peso.

events.title: May U.S. goods deficit with Mexico widens to US$21.1bn
events.context: The U.S. goods deficit with Mexico was US$21.13bn in May, up from US$15.35bn in April.
events.why: The monthly U.S. Census ledger shows the bilateral goods gap widening by US$5.78bn from April.

events.title: Equifax agrees to buy Círculo de Crédito for a $750M enterprise value
events.context: The deal would give Equifax 100% of Círculo de Crédito and is expected to close in the fourth quarter, subject to regulatory approval.
events.why: The largest clean cross-border deal of the window — a bet on Mexican credit and fintech in a country where ~44% of adults are unbanked.

events.title: USMCA stays in force, but the U.S. does not extend it at the first review.
events.context: The agreement stays in force, and the three countries will review it every year until they extend it or the process reaches 2036. Mexico and the United States have another negotiating round the week of July 20.
events.why: Without an extension at the first review, the agreement moves to annual reviews. That keeps the rules governing North American trade under regular negotiation.

events.title: May remittances rebound to a 2026-high $5.6B
events.context: Banxico reports $5.611bn in remittances in May, up from $4.978bn in April and the highest monthly total so far in 2026.
events.why: The official monthly total rose by $633m from April.

events.title: Banxico keeps the policy rate at 6.50%
events.context: Banxico kept its target rate unchanged at 6.50% on June 25. The vote was unanimous.
events.why: The central monetary pivot: after two years of cuts, the board signals a prolonged pause, diverging from a Fed the market expects to ease. Minutes published July 9 reinforced it.

events.title: April economic activity rises 2.2% from a year earlier and 1.2% from March
events.context: INEGI reports that economic activity rose 2.2% from April 2025 and 1.2% from March on a seasonally adjusted basis.
events.why: The monthly and annual comparisons both rose in April; neither one implies the same pace for the full quarter.

events.title: Government reports the daily homicide average down 46% since September 2024
events.context: The government says the daily average fell from 86.9 victims in September 2024 to 47.3 in May 2026. These are preliminary government figures; the formal monthly SESNSP release is separate.
events.why: The security narrative the whole administration rests on. Includes the lowest single day in a decade (~June 17) and the Plan Michoacán results (July 3). Note: the formal SESNSP monthly report for June isn't out until ~July 20 — these are cabinet/daily figures.

events.title: Q1 foreign direct investment reaches a record $23.591bn
events.context: The Secretaría de Economía reports $23.591bn in foreign direct investment in the first quarter, the highest first-quarter total on record.
events.why: This is the verified official first-quarter total; it does not include a separate investment-announcement tally.

events.title: Temporary 10% U.S. surcharge on non-USMCA goods runs through July 24
events.context: The surcharge took effect February 24 and runs through July 24 unless it is changed or ended earlier. Goods entering duty-free under USMCA are exempt.
events.why: The presidential proclamation sets the rate, dates, and USMCA exemption directly.

events.title: White House expands Section 232 metals tariffs (steel, aluminum, copper)
events.why: Mexico is the single largest supplier of U.S. copper imports (~22%), so the 50% metals regime is a direct, concrete cost on top of the USMCA fight.

events.title: Second judicial reform published in the DOF: judicial elections pushed to 2028, Supreme Court restructured
events.context: Pending federal and local judicial elections move from 2027 to 2028, and the Supreme Court's structure changes. A separate decree adds foreign interference as grounds to void an election.
events.why: Postpones the pending federal and local judicial elections from 2027 to 2028 and reshapes the Court (rotating two-year presidency, two sections). A separate decree adds foreign interference as grounds to void an election. Secondary laws are moving through Congress.

events.title: Moody's lowers Mexico's sovereign rating to Baa3; outlook stable
events.context: Moody's lowered the sovereign rating one notch, from Baa2 to Baa3, and changed the outlook from negative to stable. Mexico remains investment grade.
events.why: Chiefly the cost of propping up Pemex plus structural deficits — the credit story of the moment. Standing ratings: Moody's Baa3 stable, S&P BBB negative, Fitch BBB- stable.
